item.title: Securing Kali network
item.description: Exercise 1, Chapter 07 – Securing Kali Networking # Identify all open ports on your Kali instance. Configure your Kali firewall to allow inbound TCP connections on ports 22, 80, and 443 only. Verify other ports are blocked with a utility such as netcat. Make sure these rules persist after a reboot. Reboot to check! Check the open ports: root@kali:~# netstat -tulpen root@kali:~# iptables -n -L INPUT If you have ports you blocked, or previous iptables rules, you can drop them all:
